gene_region.label: MSH2 transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical MSH2 genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window used by this app to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval begins.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: MSH2 encodes MSH2, a DNA mismatch-recognition factor that pairs with MSH6 or MSH3. This bundle emphasizes DNA mismatch recognition, microsatellite stability, replication-error repair, and Lynch syndrome biology.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local MSH2 bundle is mismatch-repair review oriented. Interpret markers through exact allele class, zygosity, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context rather than as stand-alone deterministic calls.
variant_effect_overview: MSH2 variants can alter or tag DNA mismatch recognition, microsatellite stability, replication-error repair, and Lynch syndrome biology; effect direction depends on allele class, tissue, dosage, and biological context.
Common markers, pharmacogenetic alleles, structural events, and high-impact pathogenic variants should be kept separate during interpretation.
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: MSH2 methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a DNA mismatch recognition, microsatellite stability, replication-error repair, and Lynch syndrome biology gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for MSH2.
Because MSH2 biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.
Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against DNA mismatch recognition, microsatellite stability, replication-error repair, and Lynch syndrome biology.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use MSH2 promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for DNA mismatch recognition, microsatellite stability, replication-error repair, and Lynch syndrome biology.

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000095002: MSH2 coordinate source

biorender_visuals.label: MSH2 biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: MSH2: DNA mismatch recognition, microsatellite stability, replication-error repair, and Lynch syndrome biology
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a MSH2 mismatch-repair review thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to DNA mismatch recognition, microsatellite stability, replication-error repair, and Lynch syndrome biology and Lynch syndrome review context, Microsatellite instability biology. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support.

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: MSH2 loss-of-function model is curated as a MSH2 marker for Lynch syndrome review context, Microsatellite instability biology. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the DNA mismatch recognition, microsatellite stability, replication-error repair, and Lynch syndrome biology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Curated research and clinical-context marker; external clinical classification is required for diagnostic use.
functional_effects: Can modify or tag DNA mismatch recognition, microsatellite stability, replication-error repair, and Lynch syndrome biology.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
research_context: Use this marker as a MSH2 pathway-context signal, not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Sequence, methylation, expression, and protein data may point in different directions and should be reconciled.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Pair MSH2 loss-of-function model with MSH2 methylation only as local regulatory context; methylation does not prove the variant's biochemical effect.
